contentBlocks.original:
शंकरं शंकराचार्यं केशवं बादरायणम्।
सुत्र भाश्य कृतौ वन्दे भगवन्तौ पुनः पुनः॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaṅkaraṃ śaṅkarācāryaṃ keśavaṃ bādarāyaṇam.
sutra bhāśya kṛtau vande bhagavantau punaḥ punaḥ..
contentBlocks.translation: Salutations again and again to Lord Shiva in the form of Sri Sankaracharya and Lord Vishnu in the form of Veda Vyasa, who were the authors of sutra and bhasya.
contentBlocks.meanings: शंकरं (śaṅkaraṃ) – Shiva; शंकराचार्यं (śaṅkarācāryaṃ) – Sankaracharya (śaṅkara:Shiva | ācārya:teacher); केशवं (keśavaṃ) – Vishnu; बादरायणम् (bādarāyaṇam) – Veda Vyasa; सुत्र (sutra) – sutra; भाश्य (bhāśya) – commentary; कृतौ (kṛtau) – authors; वन्दे (vande) – salutations; भगवन्तौ (bhagavantau) – to the divine ones; पुनः (punaḥ) – again; पुनः (punaḥ) – again;
